rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical anti-Markovnikov hydroxyselenylation of aryl allyl ethers/allyl benzoates with diaryl diselenides to give β-hydroxy selenides under additive-free constant-current electrolysis
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diphenyl diselenide (PhSeSePh) at graphite anode to generate phenylselenyl radical and phenylselenyl cation via a diselenide radical cation; cathode (Pt) reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: β-(phenylselanyl) carbon radical adduct (IV) from PhSe• addition to allyl ether
β-(phenylselanyl) benzylic/secondary carbocation (V) after one-electron anodic oxidation of IV
β-hydroxy selenide product 3 after water addition to V

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes PhSeSePh to a diselenide radical cation that fragments into PhSe• and PhSe+; PhSe• adds to the terminal alkene of the allyl ether generating a carbon radical, which is further anodically oxidized to a carbocation; water (present in CH3CN, ~10 equiv by Karl Fischer) attacks the cation to deliver the β-hydroxy selenide while protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.
rate_determining_step: Anodic oxidation of diphenyl diselenide (Ep ≈ 1.40 V vs Ag/AgCl, lower than substrate at 1.75 V) initiates the cycle and is implicated as the triggering step

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3 obtained in only 20% yield; TEMPO–PhSe adduct 59 ([M+H]+ = 314.1004) and TEMPO-trapped selenylated alkyl adduct 60 ([M+H]+ = 462.1906) detected by HRMS, supporting Se- and C-centered radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: 3 formed in 35%; BHT-trapped product 61 ([M+H]+ = 525.2287) detected by HRMS

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3; PhSe-DPE radical adduct 62 ([M+H]+ = 337.0473) detected, confirming PhSe•

probe_reagents.name: PhSeCl
probe_reagents.observation: Gave product 3 in 30%, consistent with PhSe+ as a competent intermediate

probe_reagents.name: diphenyl disulfide (PhSSPh)
probe_reagents.observation: No sulfur analogue 65 formed (0%), indicating S-S does not behave like Se-Se under these conditions

probe_reagents.name: O2 (balloon)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction still produced 3 in 95% with no α-seleno carbonyl byproduct, ruling out molecular O2 as the oxygen source

probe_reagents.name: H2O (Karl Fischer titration)
probe_reagents.observation: ~10 equiv water detected in CH3CN, identified as the source of the hydroxyl group in 3

notes: Cyclic voltammetry shows 2a (PhSeSePh) oxidizes at 1.40 V while 1a oxidizes at 1.75 V, supporting that the diselenide—not the alkene—is oxidized first (distinct from a substrate-radical-cation pathway). PhSeCl serves as a competent PhSe+ surrogate giving 3 in 30%, supporting cation pathway V→3, while PhSSPh fails, indicating Se-specific reactivity. Water (adventitious from CH3CN) is the verified O source rather than O2.
